_comment: Magic: The Gathering franchise data file. Covers 30+ years of card game, video games, books, comics, and cancelled adaptations.
description: The world's first trading card game (1993). Wizards of the Coast's flagship brand. Expansive multiverse (the Blind Eternities) spanning dozens of planes (Ravnica, Dominaria, Innistrad). Deep lore told through cards, novels, and digital games. Hasbro-owned.

media.title: Magic: The Gathering (MicroProse)
media.description: The first digital adaptation. Includes Shandalar open-world campaign and Duels of the Planeswalkers mode. Based on 4th Edition rules.
media.notes: Classic version. Abandonware; fan patches exist to run on modern systems. Shandalar mode is still beloved for its RPG elements.

media.title: Magic: The Gathering — BattleMage
media.description: Action-adventure spinoff. Play as a mage in the city of Shiv. Real-time combat with creature summoning.
media.notes: Chronologically set in the Weatherlight Saga era. Considered a commercial failure. Emulation required.

media.title: Magic: The Gathering — Armageddon
media.description: Cancelled action-RPG. Early 3D fighter/mage hybrid. Prototype exists but was never commercially released.
media.notes: CANCELLED. Only playable via leaked prototype. Developed during the 90s Magic game boom.

media.title: Magic: The Gathering — Duels of the Planeswalkers (Xbox)
media.description: Revived the digital game. Arcade-style rules, deck unlocks, and online play. Kickstarted the modern era of MTG digital.
media.notes: Xbox Live Arcade (XBLA) / PSN. Delisted in 2014 after servers shut down. Playable via physical disc (PC) or emulation.

media.title: Magic: The Gathering — Duels of the Planeswalkers 2012
media.description: Sequel. Improved interface, Archenemy mode. Introduced the 'Duels' series formula.
media.notes: Delisted. iOS version is defunct. Physical PC version exists. Emulation via RPCS3.

media.title: Magic: The Gathering — Duels of the Planeswalkers 2013
media.description: Planechase mode added. Sealed deck play introduced.
media.notes: Delisted. Mobile versions defunct.

media.title: Magic: The Gathering — Duels of the Planeswalkers 2014
media.description: Theros expansion. 'Sealed Play' fully featured. Last of the Stainless Games series.
media.notes: Delisted. Mobile versions defunct.

media.title: Magic Duels: Origins
media.description: Free-to-play model. Story-based campaign following the Origins 5 (Gideon, Jace, Liliana, Chandra, Nissa).
media.notes: DEFUNCT. Servers shut down in 2019. Removed from Steam and app stores. Single-player only via offline mode (PC).

media.title: Magic: The Gathering Arena
media.developer: Wizards of the Coast (Digital Games Studio)
media.description: Current flagship digital client. Full rules engine. Standard, Historic, Alchemy, Limited formats. Modern UI.
media.notes: Active as of 2026. PC and Mac via Epic/Steam/Standalone. Mobile version (iOS/Android) launched 2021.

media.title: Magic: The Gathering Arena (Mobile)
media.description: Mobile port of Arena. Touch-optimized UI. Cross-progression with PC.
media.notes: Active as of 2026. Heavier on battery life.

media.title: Magic: The Gathering — Puzzle Quest
media.description: Match-3 RPG hybrid. Collect Planeswalkers and cards. Gacha mechanics. Still receiving updates as of 2026.
media.notes: Active. Free-to-play. Cross-platform progression via D3 Go account.

media.title: Magic: The Gathering — ManaStrike
media.description: Real-time strategy (RTS) spinoff. Clash Royale style. Summon creatures to destroy enemy towers.
media.notes: DEFUNCT. Servers shut down in 2022 after less than 2 years. Licensed by WotC. No longer playable.

media.title: Magic: Legends
media.description: Diablo-style action-RPG MMO. Players create a Planeswalker and travel between realms.
media.notes: CANCELLED. Open beta launched March 2021; servers shut down October 2021. Unreleased on consoles. One of the highest-profile MTG game cancellations.

media.title: Magic: The Gathering — Spellslingers
media.description: Streamlined, faster-paced card game. Simplified mana system. Designed by Ben Brode (Hearthstone).
media.notes: DEFUNCT. Servers shut down in 2024. Unique art style, tie-in comics. No longer playable.

media.title: Magic: The Gathering — TCG (Tabletop Simulator)
media.description: Not an official game, but the primary way the community plays on TTS. Scripted tables automate some mechanics.
media.notes: Unofficial but widely used. Available via Steam Workshop.

media.title: Magic: The Gathering (Netflix Series)
media.studio: Wizards of the Coast / Netflix Animation
media.description: Planned adult-animated series. Russo Brothers (Joe and Anthony) as showrunners. Focus on Gideon, Jace, Liliana, Chandra, Nissa.
media.notes: CANCELLED. Announced 2019. Officially shelved by Netflix in 2022 before release. No episodes ever aired.

media.title: Magic: The Gathering — The Musical (CollegeHumor)
media.description: Fan musical parody. 'The Gathering: A Magic: The Gathering Musical' by the CollegeHumor cast. Spoof of high fantasy and competitive TCG culture.
media.notes: Significant fan work. Widely regarded as a high-production-value cultural touchstone in the MTG community. Available on YouTube.

media.title: Magic: The Gathering — Untitled Film
media.description: Planned live-action film. Simon Kinberg (X-Men) attached as writer/producer. Focus on the Gatewatch.
media.notes: CANCELLED. Announced 2014. Left development hell and was replaced by the Netflix series, which was also cancelled.

media.title: Magic: The Gathering (Acclaim Comics)
media.description: First comic series. Published by Acclaim Comics (Armada imprint). Anthology stories featuring Garth, Serra, and other early lore characters.
media.notes: G1 era. Out of print. Darker tone. Some issues reprinted in 'Magic: The Gathering Omnibus' (IDW, 2017).

media.title: Magic: The Gathering (Dark Horse Comics)
media.description: The 'Ice Age' and 'Mirage' era comics. Anthology series focusing on Jodah, Gerda, and the lore of Terisiare.
media.notes: G1. Published by Dark Horse Comics. Out of print.

media.title: Magic: The Gathering — The Shadow Mage
media.description: One-shot graphic novel. Story centered around Ravidel and the plane of Corondor. Bridge between early lore and Invasion block.
media.notes: Published by Armada. Out of print. Considered part of the Weatherlight Saga era.

media.title: Magic: The Gathering (IDW Publishing)
media.description: Modern IDW series. Covers the 'Return to Ravnica' and 'Theros' arcs. Dack Fayden (the 'greatest thief in the Multiverse') as protagonist.
media.notes: G4. 4 trade paperbacks (25 issues). Out of print. Collected as 'Magic: The Gathering — The Complete Collection Vol. 1-4'.

media.title: Magic: The Gathering — Boom! Studios Series
media.description: Current ongoing series (as of 2026). Launched under the 'Magic' branding. Follows Planeswalkers Kaya, Ral Zarek, and Vraska on Ravnica.
media.notes: Active. Published by Boom! Studios. Issues #1–25 currently collected. Tie-ins with recent sets (March of the Machine, Outlaws of Thunder Junction).

media.title: Magic: The Gathering — The Hidden Planeswalker
media.description: Boom! Studios one-shot. Focus on the mystery of the 'hidden' planeswalker. Tie-in to the 'Streets of New Capenna' set.
media.notes: One-shot. Canon.

media.title: The Vorthos Cast
media.description: Fan podcast dedicated to MTG lore. Deep dives into storylines, characters, and continuity. Longest-running fan lore resource.
media.notes: Unofficial. Highly influential in the community. Not canon but a significant preservation project.

media.title: Magic: The Gathering — Voice of All (Audio Drama)
media.description: Official audio drama for the 'Guilds of Ravnica' block. Produced by WotC. Narrated by a journalist investigating the Guildpact.
media.notes: Official WotC release. Available on YouTube and podcast platforms. 10 episodes.

media.title: Magic: The Gathering — Wilds of Eldraine: The Lost Realms
media.description: Official audio drama podcast. Ties into the Wilds of Eldraine set. Follows Kellan and the fae of the plane.
media.notes: Official WotC release. Available on Spotify, Apple Podcasts. 8 episodes.

media.title: Arena (Magic: The Gathering Novel)
media.description: First MTG novel. Written by William R. Forstchen. Set in the early 'Arena' setting. Garth One-Eye protagonist.
media.notes: G1. HarperPrism. Standalone, considered semi-canon.

media.title: The Brothers' War
media.description: Cornerstone MTG novel. Written by Jeff Grubb. Covers the Urza/Mishra conflict and the fall of Argoth. Part of the Artifacts Cycle.
media.notes: Artifacts Cycle #1. High canon. Later reprinted as 'The Artifacts Cycle I'.

media.title: Planeswalker
media.description: Artifacts Cycle #2. Written by Lynn Abbey. Follows Urza post-Brothers' War and his journey to Serra's Realm.
media.notes: Canon. Out of print. Critical to understanding Urza's character.

media.title: Time Streams
media.description: Artifacts Cycle #3. Written by J. Robert King. Covers Urza's Academy and the creation of the Legacy.
media.notes: Canon. Out of print.

media.title: Bloodlines: The Story of Urza's Destiny
media.description: Artifacts Cycle #4. Written by Loren L. Coleman. End of the Urza arc before the Invasion.
media.notes: Canon. Out of print.

media.title: The Thran
media.description: Written by J. Robert King. Prequel to The Brothers' War. Covers the fall of the Thran empire and Yawgmoth's rise.
media.notes: Canon. One of the most critically acclaimed MTG novels.

media.title: Invasion (Invasion Cycle #1)
media.description: Start of the Invasion block. Written by J. Robert King. Phyrexian invasion of Dominaria begins.
media.notes: Canon. Weatherlight Saga finale.

media.title: Planeshift (Invasion Cycle #2)
media.description: Written by J. Robert King. The war shifts to Rath and Phyrexia.
media.notes: Canon.

media.title: Apocalypse (Invasion Cycle #3)
media.description: Conclusion of the Weatherlight Saga. Written by J. Robert King. Climax of the Urza/Yawgmoth arc.
media.notes: Canon.

media.title: The Moons of Mirrodin (Mirrodin Cycle #1)
media.description: Written by Will McDermott. Introduction of the Mirrodin plane. Glissa Sunseeker protagonist.
media.notes: Canon.

media.title: The Darksteel Eye (Mirrodin Cycle #2)
media.description: Written by Jess Lebow.
media.notes: Canon.

media.title: The Fifth Dawn (Mirrodin Cycle #3)
media.description: Written by Cory J. Herndon.
media.notes: Canon.

media.title: Agents of Artifice
media.description: First 'Planeswalker' novel. Written by Ari Marmell. Focus on Jace Beleren and Liliana Vess. Alara block tie-in.
media.notes: Canon. The start of the 'modern' Gatewatch era novels.

media.title: The Purifying Fire
media.description: Written by Laura Resnick. Chandra Nalaar vs the Order of Heliud.
media.notes: Canon.

media.title: Test of Metal
media.description: Written by Matthew Stover. Focus on Tezzeret. Scars of Mirrodin tie-in.
media.notes: Canon.

media.title: The Secretist (Return to Ravnica e-book trilogy)
media.description: Digital-first e-book trilogy. Written by Doug Beyer. Focus on Jace Beleren on Ravnica. Part 1: 'The Secretist', Part 2: 'Guilds of Ravnica', Part 3: 'Dragon's Maze'.
media.notes: Canon. Available as a single print volume (2013).

media.title: Theros: Godsend (E-book series)
media.description: Digital novel by Jenna Helland. Focus on Elspeth Tirel and the god Heliod. Part 1: 'Godsend', Part 2: 'Journey into Nyx'.
media.notes: Canon. Available as a single volume.

media.title: The Art of Magic: The Gathering — Zendikar (Art Book)
media.description: Art book series by James Wyatt. Expansive lore text. Zendikar, Innistrad, Kaladesh, Ravnica, and War of the Spark volumes.
media.notes: High canon reference material. Published by Viz Media.

media.title: War of the Spark: Ravnica
media.description: Novel by Greg Weisman. Covers the Bolas arc climax.
media.notes: Canon. Mixed reception due to plot contradictions.

media.title: War of the Spark: Forsaken
media.description: Sequel novel. Controversial for its handling of character arcs (Ral Zarek, Dack Fayden's fate).
media.notes: Canon (but widely disputed by fans).

media.title: Eldraine: The Wildered Quest
media.description: E-book by Kate Elliott. Introduces the plane of Eldraine. Follows Garruk, Will, and Rowan Kenrith.
media.notes: Canon. High quality prose.

media.title: The Gathering Storm (Pre-War of the Spark)
media.description: Digital serial novel. Bridge between Dominaria and War of the Spark. Focus on Ral Zarek's investigation.
media.notes: Canon. Available on the MTG website archive.

media.title: Children of the Nameless
media.description: Free e-book by Brandon Sanderson. Set on the plane of Innistrad. Davriel Cane protagonist.
media.notes: Canon. Sanderson's only MTG work. Removed from free distribution in 2020 due to licensing; now rare to find legally.
